title: coleostephus paludosus
intro: Coleostephus paludosus, also known as the Marsh Daisy or Marsh Cudweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Coleostephus paludosus often involve drying the leaves and flowers to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
